heading: Annual reporting to prevent fraud and abuse
label: 283a–1 Annual reporting to prevent fraud and abuse
notes: Amendments2016—Subsecs. (b), (c). redesignated subsec. (c) as (b), substituted “subsection (a)” for “subsections (a) and (b)”, and struck out former subsec. (b), which related to annual report by the Director of NIH on experts and consultants whose services were obtained by the National Institutes of Health or its agencies.Pub. L. 114–255

Effective DateSection applicable only with respect to amounts appropriated for fiscal year 2007 or subsequent fiscal years, see , set out as an Effective Date of 2007 Amendment note under .section 109 of Pub. L. 109–482section 281 of this title
amendments: Amendments2016—Subsecs. (b), (c). redesignated subsec. (c) as (b), substituted “subsection (a)” for “subsections (a) and (b)”, and struck out former subsec. (b), which related to annual report by the Director of NIH on experts and consultants whose services were obtained by the National Institutes of Health or its agencies.Pub. L. 114–255

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: On an annual basis, the Director of NIH shall submit to the Inspector General of the Department of Health and Human Services, the Secretary, the Committee on Energy and Commerce and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives, and the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate a report summarizing the activities of the National Institutes of Health relating to whistleblower complaints.

children.label: A
children.text: Each agency of the National Institutes of Health involved.

children.label: C
children.text: The resolution of the complaint to date.

children.heading: First report
children.label: b First report
children.text: The first report under subsection (a) shall be submitted not later than 1 year after .January 15, 2007
